title: Using Third-Party for Creating Automations

posts.author: admindept (moonui)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!

We’re a small made-to-order furniture company selling on Shopify and using Monday for our admin tasks.

Currently, we manually input our Shopify order details into a Google Spreadsheet in order to generate “cut-lists” for our wood shop (ex: someone orders a LARGE cabinet, we input that order into our sheet and it spits our all the parts required for us to make a LARGE cabinet).

We also manually input our Shopify order details into a Monday board- making sure each item includes the customer name, cabinet ordered, and “ship-by” date (a date 15 weeks from their order date).

We also use Shipstation to ship everyone’s orders.

What’s the best app to go about creating these automations? Is this fully possible?

posts.author: JHconsultancy (Joseph Honig)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

You can use make.com to do all of this.

  1. When a order comes in from Shopify create/input the data to Google Sheets and monday.com
  2. When order is ready to be shipped update shipstation from monday.com

Hope this helps

Feel free to DM me if you need further assistance

posts.author: admindept (moonui)
posts.cookedHtml:
Got it! Thank you!

Is Make.com the best option for this? How’s it preferable to, say, Zapier?

posts.author: Carson (Carson Butcher)
posts.cookedHtml:
It all comes down to preference, but both can achieve a similar outcome.

Make.com is great for complex, multi-step automations and offers deep customization, ideal for intricate workflows. Zapier is user-friendly, with a wide range of pre-made integrations, making it suitable for simple, straightforward automations, especially for beginners.

If you’re looking for help building out your solution we’ve got an awesome team of solutions architects and API developers to help bring this workflow to life! I think we could even simplify your process entirely with the right setup, shopify→monday→shipstation, cutting out google sheets if you’d want to.

If you’re interested, book a free workflow exploration with us!

posts.author: GiannisKoukounas (Giannis Koukounas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @admindept

Welcome to the monday community !

I am sure our team at thespelas.com can help you out!

We have worked on this exact case with other clients, integrating Shopify/Shipstation with monday, so when an order is placed, we automatically create the order on monday and the parts of the order ( in your case the parts required to create a large cabinet for example) , as well as automatically assigning the people responsible with deadlines ( where applicable).

Within this framework, we can also help you track all of your orders and their progress, and additionally offer useful KPIs if desired.

Please do schedule an initial Free Consulting Session with us here so we can better discuss your vision and requirements! Alternatively, if none of the available time slots work out for you, please contact us at info@thespelas.com so we can do our best to accommodate you.

I sincerely hope our services can be of use to you!

Best Regards,
Giannis Koukounas, Implementation Consultant at thespelas.com
